title: Partition a String into Palindromes
description:
Given Split a string into all possible lists of substrings where every substring is a palindrome. Use backtracking with two pointers to test palindromes on the fly or precompute a DP table of palindromic ranges.

Constraints:
- 1 ≤ |s| ≤ 16
explanation:
Intuition
At each index, try all end positions that form palindromes and recurse from the next index. The structure is a partition tree across all cuts.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Optionally precompute pal[i][j]
2. Main Algorithm: DFS from start; if s[start..end] is palindrome, append and recurse at end+1
3. Return Result: Push path when start hits n

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: Exponential in number of partitions
- Space Complexity: O(n) recursion + optional O(n^2) DP

Key Insights
- Checking palindromes in O(1) with DP speeds recursion
- Backtracking explores partition choices

Common Pitfalls
- Not copying path yields aliasing
- Off-by-one in substring slicing is common
hints: Recurse on next index after a cut
